categories.introduction: The Nut & Seed Ontology catalogs canonical culinary nuts, edible seeds, and nut- and seed-derived ingredients used worldwide in cooking. This domain models culinary ingredients, not botanical taxonomy, snack products, confectionery, or prepared foods.
categories.name: Nuts & Seeds
categories.seo_description: Explore canonical culinary nuts and edible seeds by group — usage intensity, identity, and pairing metadata in the PairingMethod Food Ontology.
categories.summary: Canonical culinary nuts and seeds across six immutable groups: Tree Nuts, Peanuts, Edible Seeds, Seed Spices, Nut Products, and Seed Products.

groups.introduction: Tree nuts in the Nut & Seed Ontology — culinary tree nuts used whole, chopped, and roasted in cooking and baking, including almond, walnut, pecan, hazelnut, pistachio, cashew, and macadamia.
groups.name: Tree Nuts
groups.seo_description: Canonical tree nuts — almond, walnut, pecan, and related culinary ingredients in the PairingMethod Nut & Seed Ontology.
groups.summary: Tree Nuts — 21 canonical nut and seed ingredients in the Nut & Seed Ontology.

groups.introduction: Peanuts in the Nut & Seed Ontology — the culinary peanut group. Despite botanical legume status, peanut is classified here by established culinary identity per NUT-001.
groups.name: Peanuts
groups.seo_description: Canonical peanuts — culinary peanut ingredients in the PairingMethod Nut & Seed Ontology.
groups.summary: Peanuts — 1 canonical nut and seed ingredients in the Nut & Seed Ontology.

groups.introduction: Edible seeds in the Nut & Seed Ontology — whole seeds used in cooking, baking, and garnish, including sesame, sunflower seed, pumpkin seed, flaxseed, and chia seed.
groups.name: Edible Seeds
groups.seo_description: Canonical edible seeds — sesame, sunflower seed, pumpkin seed, and related culinary ingredients in the PairingMethod Nut & Seed Ontology.
groups.summary: Edible Seeds — 24 canonical nut and seed ingredients in the Nut & Seed Ontology.

groups.introduction: Seed spices in the Nut & Seed Ontology — edible seeds with primary spice-like culinary function not governed by the Herb & Spice Ontology. Mustard seed and other Herb & Spice entities are excluded.
groups.name: Seed Spices
groups.seo_description: Canonical seed spices — nigella, caraway seed, and related culinary seed ingredients in the PairingMethod Nut & Seed Ontology.
groups.summary: Seed Spices — 9 canonical nut and seed ingredients in the Nut & Seed Ontology.

groups.introduction: Nut products in the Nut & Seed Ontology — processed nut forms with independent culinary identity per NUT-002, including nut flours, nut butters, and nut meals.
groups.name: Nut Products
groups.seo_description: Canonical nut products — almond flour, peanut butter, and related processed nut ingredients in the PairingMethod Nut & Seed Ontology.
groups.summary: Nut Products — 18 canonical nut and seed ingredients in the Nut & Seed Ontology.

groups.introduction: Seed products in the Nut & Seed Ontology — processed seed forms with independent culinary identity per NUT-002, including tahini and other governed seed pastes and flours.
groups.name: Seed Products
groups.seo_description: Canonical seed products — tahini, seed flours, and related processed seed ingredients in the PairingMethod Nut & Seed Ontology.
groups.summary: Seed Products — 16 canonical nut and seed ingredients in the Nut & Seed Ontology.

meta.description: Nut & Seed Ontology — canonical catalog populated (FOOD-10B). Intrinsic metadata only; relationships deferred to FOOD-10D/E.

aroma_profile.description: Reserved aroma characteristic tags — vocabulary frozen in FOOD-10A; do not populate until post-FOOD-10B curation standards.

canonical_culinary_groups.description: Immutable group names and slugs — do not rename or add peer groups in v1.

culinary_group.description: Canonical Culinary Group value on leaf entities — aligns to immutable group slugs.

flavor_profile.description: Reserved flavor characteristic tags — vocabulary frozen in FOOD-10A; do not populate until post-FOOD-10B curation standards.

texture_profile.description: Reserved texture characteristic tags — vocabulary frozen in FOOD-10A; do not populate until post-FOOD-10B curation standards.

usage_intensity.description: Intrinsic culinary prominence in dish composition — not Pairing Engine reasoning.

domain_exclusions.beverages: Plant milks and nut/seed beverages — reserved; out of domain v1
domain_exclusions.herbs_spices: FOOD-07 — mustard seed and whole spice entities
domain_exclusions.note: Entities in these domains are cross-referenced by canonical ID only — never duplicated intrinsically.
domain_exclusions.prepared_foods: Candy, trail mix, breakfast products, and snack products — not ingredient ontology

nut_seed.description: Canonical culinary nut, edible seed, or nut/seed-derived ingredient with frozen intrinsic metadata.

nut_seed_category.description: Top-level nut & seed domain hub (single category in v1).

nut_seed_group.description: Canonical Culinary Group (Tree Nuts, Edible Seeds, Nut Products, etc.).

botanical_ownership_rule.note: Suite rule — applies when botanical lineage diverges from culinary identity; complements NUT-001.

culinary_classification_rule.description: Group assignment follows established culinary identity, not botanical taxonomy alone.

culinary_form_ownership_rule.description: Cross-domain fruit processed forms — referenced for coconut ownership; does not govern Nut & Seed entities.

functional_pairing_rule.note: Suite wine-pairing rule — referenced for completeness; FOOD-10E may introduce domain pairing rule.

processed_product_rule.description: A processed nut or seed becomes a separate canonical ingredient only when it has an independent culinary identity.

processing_ownership_rule.description: When processing creates a distinct canonical culinary ingredient versus a preparation state or alias.

schema.taxonomy_note: Groups represent culinary identity and culinary form, not botanical families. See docs/NUT_SEED_GOVERNANCE.md.
